Header file for serial debugging functions, including initialization, character output, and baud rate control.

# Purpose
This C header file defines the interface for a serial debugging utility. It provides function declarations for initializing the debugging serial interface with [`dbgserial_init`](<#dbgserial_init>), sending individual characters with [`dbgserial_putchar`](<#dbgserial_putchar>), and sending strings with [`dbgserial_putstr`](<#dbgserial_putstr>). The [`dbgserial_putchar_lazy`](<#dbgserial_putchar_lazy>) function allows non-blocking character transmission, while [`dbgserial_putstr_fmt`](<#dbgserial_putstr_fmt>) supports formatted string output similar to `printf`. The file also includes functions to change and restore the baud rate of the serial connection with [`dbgserial_change_baud_rate`](<#dbgserial_change_baud_rate>) and [`dbgserial_restore_baud_rate`](<#dbgserial_restore_baud_rate>), respectively. The [`dbgserial_flush`](<#dbgserial_flush>) function ensures that all characters are written to the output before proceeding. The header includes standard libraries `stdbool.h` and `stdint.h` for boolean and integer type definitions.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdbool.h`
- `stdint.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### dbgserial\_change\_baud\_rate<!-- {{#callable_declaration:dbgserial_change_baud_rate}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/dbgserial.h#L35>)

Changes the baud rate of the debug serial interface.
- **Description**: Use this function to modify the baud rate of the debug serial interface to a new value. This is useful when you need to adjust the communication speed for different operational requirements or to match the baud rate of other devices. Ensure that the new baud rate is supported by both the hardware and the connected devices to avoid communication errors.
- **Inputs**:
    - `new_baud`: The new baud rate to set for the debug serial interface. It must be a valid baud rate supported by the hardware. Invalid values may result in undefined behavior or communication errors.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`dbgserial_change_baud_rate`](<dbgserial.c.md#dbgserial_change_baud_rate>)  (Implementation)


---
### dbgserial\_restore\_baud\_rate<!-- {{#callable_declaration:dbgserial_restore_baud_rate}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/dbgserial.h#L39>)

Restores the baud rate to the default value.
- **Description**: Use this function to reset the baud rate of the debug serial interface to its default setting. This is useful after changing the baud rate with `dbgserial_change_baud_rate`. Ensure that the debug serial interface is initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`dbgserial_restore_baud_rate`](<dbgserial.c.md#dbgserial_restore_baud_rate>)  (Implementation)


---
### dbgserial\_flush<!-- {{#callable_declaration:dbgserial_flush}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/dbgserial.h#L42>)

Finish writing all characters to the debug serial output.
- **Description**: Use this function to ensure that all characters in the transmission buffer are sent to the debug serial output. This function is useful when you need to guarantee that all data has been transmitted before proceeding, such as before shutting down the system or changing configurations. It must be called after characters have been queued for transmission using other functions in this API.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`dbgserial_flush`](<dbgserial.c.md#dbgserial_flush>)  (Implementation)
